core_thesis: Heidegger reopens the question of Being by distinguishing Being from beings and taking Dasein, the entity for whom Being is at issue, as the proper site of inquiry ([Being and Time §2](cite://heidegger/being-and-time/2); [§4](cite://heidegger/being-and-time/4)). Dasein is not a detached subject but being-in-the-world, disclosed through care and being-toward-death ([§12](cite://heidegger/being-and-time/12); [§53](cite://heidegger/being-and-time/53)). Truth is first unconcealment rather than correspondence, so thought must attend to the clearing in which beings appear ([§44](cite://heidegger/being-and-time/44); [Parmenides, 'Aletheia'](cite://heidegger/parmenides/aletheia)).
